cost_name: PEM Stack Learning Curve Projection — 2030 — 100 MW — Global Average

context.extrapolation_method: Learning curve projection from 2025 baseline (€800/kW) applying 15% learning rate. Cumulative PEM capacity: 4.5 GW (2025) → ~18 GW (2030) = 2.0 doublings. Cost_2030 = 800 × (1 - 0.15)^2.0 = 800 × 0.722 = €578/kW. Rounded to €620/kW central with wider range for forecasting uncertainty.

estimation.estimation_method: Learning curve projection using IEA-documented 15% learning rate. Cumulative capacity projections from IEA STEPS scenario.

confidence.rationale: Forward projection based on learning curve methodology. Actual 2030 cost depends on iridium loading reduction success, gigafactory ramp-up timing, OEM competition evolution, and demand/supply balance. Wide uncertainty range reflects forecasting risk.

primary_drivers: Iridium loading reduction from 0.3-0.5 → <0.1 g/kW (critical uncertainty)
Gigafactory manufacturing scale (Siemens Energy 3 GW, ITM Power 1.5 GW, Plug Power 1 GW)
Membrane thickness reduction and higher current density
OEM competitive dynamics (new entrants at >20 MW scale)
exclusions: This is a FORECAST, not a benchmark — use with appropriate caution

sources.title: IEA GHR 2025 — Learning rate analysis and capacity projections
sources.author: IEA

sources.title: IRENA 2024 — PEM cost reduction trajectory to 2030
sources.author: IRENA
